test_cases.description: Verify that cmd/akc/main.go registers all five command groups on the root command via explicit AddCommand calls and uses no init() functions.
preconditions: Go source tree is available under cmd/akc/main.go and internal/cli/
All constructor functions (NewLoginCmd, NewUserCmd, NewKeysCmd, NewTokensCmd, NewOrgsCmd) are implemented
test_cases.input: Execute the compiled akc binary with no arguments or with --help
test_cases.expected: Root command's Commands() slice contains exactly login, user, keys, tokens, and orgs sub-commands; no init() registration side effects occur
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: root := clicore.NewRootCmd(); root.AddCommand(cli.NewLoginCmd(), cli.NewUserCmd(), cli.NewKeysCmd(), cli.NewTokensCmd(), cli.NewOrgsCmd()); names := [cmd.Name() for cmd in root.Commands()]; assert names contains 'login', 'user', 'keys', 'tokens', 'orgs'; assert len(names) == 5

test_cases.description: Verify that every Cobra command constructor populates Use, Short, Long, Args, RunE, flag definitions, and help-json annotations on the returned command.
preconditions: All constructor functions are implemented in internal/cli/
test_cases.input: Call each constructor function and inspect the returned *cobra.Command
test_cases.expected: Each *cobra.Command has non-empty Use, Short, Long; RunE is non-nil; help-json annotations contain name, description, method, path, args, flags, auth fields
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for each constructor in [NewLoginCmd, NewUserShowCmd, NewUserUpdateCmd, NewKeysListCmd, NewKeysRefreshCmd, NewKeysRevokeCmd, NewTokensListCmd, NewTokensCreateCmd, NewTokensShowCmd, NewTokensRevokeCmd, NewOrgsListCmd, NewOrgsShowCmd, NewOrgsMembersCmd]: cmd = constructor(); assert cmd.Use != ''; assert cmd.Short != ''; assert cmd.RunE != nil; assert cmd.Annotations['help-json'] contains 'name', 'description', 'method', 'path', 'auth'

test_cases.description: Verify that the login command's help-json annotation sets composite=true, method=null, path=null, and auth='none'.
preconditions: NewLoginCmd() is implemented in internal/cli/login.go
test_cases.input: Call NewLoginCmd() and inspect its Annotations map
test_cases.expected: help-json annotation has composite=true, method=null, path=null, auth='none'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: cmd = NewLoginCmd(); meta = parse_json(cmd.Annotations['help-json']); assert meta['composite'] == true; assert meta['method'] == null; assert meta['path'] == null; assert meta['auth'] == 'none'

test_cases.description: Verify that all non-login commands have auth='api_key' and non-null HTTP method and path in their help-json annotations.
preconditions: All non-login command constructors are implemented in internal/cli/
test_cases.input: Call each non-login constructor and inspect its Annotations map
test_cases.expected: Each non-login command has auth='api_key', method is a non-null HTTP verb string, path is a non-null API path string
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for each constructor in [NewUserShowCmd, NewUserUpdateCmd, NewKeysListCmd, NewKeysRefreshCmd, NewKeysRevokeCmd, NewTokensListCmd, NewTokensCreateCmd, NewTokensShowCmd, NewTokensRevokeCmd, NewOrgsListCmd, NewOrgsShowCmd, NewOrgsMembersCmd]: cmd = constructor(); meta = parse_json(cmd.Annotations['help-json']); assert meta['auth'] == 'api_key'; assert meta['method'] != null; assert meta['path'] != null

test_cases.description: Verify that the login command accepts --provider flag defaulting to 'github' and --expires flag defaulting to 90.
preconditions: NewLoginCmd() is implemented
test_cases.input: Call NewLoginCmd() and inspect flag definitions without setting any flags
test_cases.expected: providerFlag.DefValue == 'github'; expiresFlag.DefValue == '90'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: cmd = NewLoginCmd(); providerFlag = cmd.Flags().Lookup('provider'); assert providerFlag != nil; assert providerFlag.DefValue == 'github'; expiresFlag = cmd.Flags().Lookup('expires'); assert expiresFlag != nil; assert expiresFlag.DefValue == '90'

test_cases.description: Verify that the login command constructs an unauthenticated SDK client and calls GetProviders when invoked with a valid endpoint_url.
preconditions: httptest.Server is configured to handle GET /providers and return a providers list
No api_key is configured (login is unauthenticated)
endpoint_url is set to the httptest.Server URL
test_cases.input: Call runLogin with valid provider='github', expires=90, endpoint_url=mockServerURL and a short timeout
test_cases.expected: Mock server receives GET /providers request; response is parsed without error; flow proceeds to provider lookup
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_returns_providers); opts = loginOpts{provider: 'github', expires: 90, endpointURL: server.URL}; err = runLogin(ctx, 200ms, opts); assert server.requestLog contains 'GET /providers'; assert err is not 'endpoint URL required' error

test_cases.description: Verify that the login command exits with code 1 and provider-not-found error JSON when the requested provider is absent from the server's list.
preconditions: httptest.Server returns a providers list that does NOT include 'gitlab'
endpoint_url is set to the mock server URL
test_cases.input: Run login command with --provider gitlab against the mock server
test_cases.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":404,"message":"provider 'gitlab' not found"}}; exit code is 1
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_returns_providers_without_gitlab); stdout, stderr, exitCode = run_login_cmd(provider='gitlab', endpointURL=server.URL); assert exitCode == 1; parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed['error']['code'] == 404; assert parsed['error']['message'] == "provider 'gitlab' not found"

test_cases.description: Verify that the generated OAuth state parameter is exactly 64 lowercase hex characters derived from 32 random bytes.
preconditions: internal/cli/helpers.go or login.go state generation function is available
test_cases.input: Call the state generation logic (crypto/rand.Read 32 bytes, hex.EncodeToString)
test_cases.expected: Returned string has length 64; contains only characters [0-9a-f]; different calls produce different values
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: state1 = generateState(); assert len(state1) == 64; assert state1 matches regex '^[0-9a-f]{64}$'; state2 = generateState(); assert state1 != state2

test_cases.description: Verify that the callback server binds to 127.0.0.1 on a random port and returns HTTP 404 for any path other than /callback.
preconditions: Login flow is initialized with a mock provider
Callback server is started as part of runLogin
test_cases.input: Send GET /favicon.ico to the running callback server's address
test_cases.expected: HTTP response status is 404; neither codeCh nor errCh receives a value; server continues listening
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: codeCh = make(chan string, 1); errCh = make(chan error, 1); server, addr = startCallbackServer(state, codeCh, errCh); resp = http_get('http://' + addr + '/favicon.ico'); assert resp.StatusCode == 404; select { case <-codeCh: fail('unexpected code'); case <-errCh: fail('unexpected error'); default: pass }

test_cases.description: Verify that the authorization URL preserves existing query parameters from authorize_url and appends redirect_uri, state, and response_type=code without duplicating client_id.
preconditions: Provider authorize_url is 'https://auth.example.com/oauth?client_id=abc123&scope=read'
test_cases.input: Call the authorization URL construction logic with redirectURI='http://127.0.0.1:12345/callback', state='deadbeef...', existing authorize_url
test_cases.expected: Final URL contains client_id=abc123, scope=read, redirect_uri=http://127.0.0.1:12345/callback, state=deadbeef..., response_type=code; client_id appears exactly once
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: authorizeURL = 'https://auth.example.com/oauth?client_id=abc123&scope=read'; state = 'deadbeef'; redirectURI = 'http://127.0.0.1:12345/callback'; finalURL = buildAuthURL(authorizeURL, redirectURI, state); params = parse_query(finalURL); assert params['client_id'] == 'abc123'; assert params['scope'] == 'read'; assert params['redirect_uri'] == redirectURI; assert params['state'] == state; assert params['response_type'] == 'code'; assert count_occurrences(finalURL, 'client_id') == 1

test_cases.description: Verify that the login command prints the browser-opening message to stderr and falls back to printing the URL if openBrowser fails.
preconditions: openBrowser is replaced with a stub that returns an error
test_cases.input: Run login flow with openBrowser stub that returns error
test_cases.expected: stderr contains 'Opening browser for authentication...'; stderr also contains 'Open this URL in your browser: <url>'; login flow continues without error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stderrBuf = buffer(); openBrowserFn = func(url) { return error('no display') }; runLoginWithStubs(stderrBuf, openBrowserFn); assert stderrBuf contains 'Opening browser for authentication...'; assert stderrBuf contains 'Open this URL in your browser:'

test_cases.description: Verify that the login flow uses runLogin with a timeout derived from loginTimeoutSeconds and that the main goroutine selects on codeCh, errCh, and ctx.Done().
preconditions: login.go defines loginTimeoutSeconds = 120 as a package-level constant
runLogin function signature is func runLogin(ctx context.Context, timeout time.Duration, opts loginOpts) error
test_cases.input: Inspect the loginTimeoutSeconds constant value and the RunE implementation that calls runLogin
test_cases.expected: loginTimeoutSeconds == 120; RunE passes time.Duration(loginTimeoutSeconds)*time.Second to runLogin; runLogin selects on codeCh, errCh, ctx.Done()
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert loginTimeoutSeconds == 120; cmd = NewLoginCmd(); // inspect RunE source or via reflection that it calls runLogin with 120s timeout; // call runLogin directly with 50ms timeout and no callback server signal; err = runLogin(ctx, 50*ms, opts); assert err.message contains 'timed out'

test_cases.description: Verify that the callback server handler responds with HTTP 200 and the verbatim success HTML page and sends the code on codeCh when a valid state and code are provided.
preconditions: Callback server is running with a known state value
codeCh and errCh channels are created
test_cases.input: Send GET /callback?state=<correct_state>&code=authcode123 to the callback server
test_cases.expected:
HTTP response status is 200; body is exactly '

Login successful

You may close this tab.

'; codeCh receives 'authcode123'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
state = 'validstate64chars'; codeCh = make(chan string, 1); errCh = make(chan error, 1); server, addr = startCallbackServer(state, codeCh, errCh); resp = http_get('http://' + addr + '/callback?state=' + state + '&code=authcode123'); assert resp.StatusCode == 200; assert resp.Body == '

Login successful

You may close this tab.

'; code = <-codeCh; assert code == 'authcode123'

test_cases.description: Verify that a state mismatch on the callback causes HTTP 400 with the error HTML page, sends an error on errCh, and results in exit code 2 with CSRF error JSON.
preconditions: Callback server is running with known state='correctstate'
codeCh and errCh channels are created
test_cases.input: Send GET /callback?state=wrongstate&code=authcode123 to the callback server
test_cases.expected: HTTP response status is 400; body is exactly the state-mismatch HTML page; errCh receives non-nil error; login command exits with code 2 and OAuth state mismatch error JSON to stdout
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
state = 'correctstate'; codeCh = make(chan string, 1); errCh = make(chan error, 1); server, addr = startCallbackServer(state, codeCh, errCh); resp = http_get('http://' + addr + '/callback?state=wrongstate&code=auth123'); assert resp.StatusCode == 400; assert resp.Body == '

Login failed

OAuth state mismatch. Please try again.

'; err = <-errCh; assert err != nil; // full command: stdout, exitCode = run_login_cmd_with_bad_state(); assert exitCode == 2; assert parse_json(stdout)['error']['message'] contains 'OAuth state mismatch'

test_cases.description: Verify that upon receiving the code from codeCh, the login command calls ExchangeOAuthCode with the correct provider, code, redirect_uri, and expires.
preconditions: Mock server handles GET /providers and POST /auth/callback
Callback server is configured to send a valid code
endpoint_url points to mock server
test_cases.input: Complete login flow up to code exchange with provider='github', expires=30, code='testcode'
test_cases.expected: Mock server receives POST to exchange endpoint with body containing provider='github', code='testcode', redirect_uri matching http://127.0.0.1:<port>/callback, expires=30
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: capturedBody = nil; server = httptest.NewServer(func(req) { if req.URL.Path == '/auth/callback': capturedBody = parse_json(req.Body); return success_response }); run_login_with_code_injection('testcode', 'github', 30, server.URL); assert capturedBody['provider'] == 'github'; assert capturedBody['code'] == 'testcode'; assert capturedBody['redirect_uri'] matches 'http://127.0.0.1:[0-9]+/callback'; assert capturedBody['expires'] == 30

test_cases.description: Verify that after a successful code exchange the login command saves endpoint_url, user_id, and api_key to config, prints user JSON to stdout, and prints the success message to stderr.
preconditions: Mock server returns a valid ExchangeOAuthCode response with user.id='user-123', user.username='alice', api_key.key='ak_keyid_secret'
Config save function (CLI Core atomic write) is available
endpoint_url is configured
test_cases.input: Complete full login flow ending in successful code exchange
test_cases.expected: Config file contains endpoint_url, user_id='user-123', api_key='ak_keyid_secret'; stdout contains user JSON with username='alice'; stderr contains 'Logged in as alice'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stdout, stderr, exitCode = run_full_login_flow(mockServer); assert exitCode == 0; config = load_config(); assert config['endpoint_url'] != ''; assert config['user_id'] == 'user-123'; assert config['api_key'] == 'ak_keyid_secret'; user = parse_json(stdout); assert user['username'] == 'alice'; assert stderr contains 'Logged in as alice'

test_cases.description: Verify that the callback server is shut down using a fresh 5-second background context after login completes.
preconditions: Full login flow is completed successfully
http.Server.Shutdown is instrumented to capture the context used
test_cases.input: Complete full login flow
test_cases.expected: Server.Shutdown is called with a context derived from context.Background() (not the login context); the context has a 5-second deadline
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: // Instrument or wrap http.Server.Shutdown to capture its context argument; shutdownCtx = captured_shutdown_ctx; assert shutdownCtx != loginCtx; deadline, ok = shutdownCtx.Deadline(); assert ok == true; assert deadline is approximately 5 seconds from now

test_cases.description: Verify that when the login context times out before a callback arrives, the command exits with code 2 and the timeout error JSON.
preconditions: runLogin is called directly with a 100ms timeout
No callback is sent to the server
test_cases.input: Call runLogin with timeout=100ms and a mock server that never sends a callback
test_cases.expected: runLogin returns an error containing 'login timed out waiting for browser callback'; exit code is 2
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: opts = loginOpts{provider: 'github', expires: 90, endpointURL: mockServerURL}; err = runLogin(context.Background(), 100*ms, opts); assert err != nil; assert err.Error() contains 'login timed out waiting for browser callback'; stdout, exitCode = run_login_cmd_with_timeout(100ms); assert exitCode == 2; parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed['error']['message'] == 'login timed out waiting for browser callback'

test_cases.description: Verify that the login command exits with code 2 and the missing endpoint URL error JSON when endpoint_url is absent.
preconditions: No endpoint_url in config file, environment variables, or flags
No api_key configured
test_cases.input: Run akc login without providing --endpoint-url, ENDPOINT_URL env var, or config file entry
test_cases.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":2,"message":"endpoint URL is required for login — use --endpoint-url or set ENDPOINT_URL"}}; exit code is 2; no API calls made
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: clearEnv('ENDPOINT_URL'); clearConfig(); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd('akc login'); assert exitCode == 2; parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed['error']['code'] == 2; assert parsed['error']['message'] == 'endpoint URL is required for login — use --endpoint-url or set ENDPOINT_URL'

test_cases.description: Verify that the login command exits with code 2 and the expires validation error JSON when --expires is an invalid value.
preconditions: endpoint_url is configured
test_cases.input: Run akc login --expires 45 --endpoint-url https://api.example.com
test_cases.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":2,"message":"--expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90"}}; exit code is 2; no API calls made
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stdout, exitCode = run_cmd('akc login --expires 45 --endpoint-url https://api.example.com'); assert exitCode == 2; parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed['error']['code'] == 2; assert parsed['error']['message'] == '--expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90'

test_cases.description: Verify that user show constructs an authenticated client, calls GetUser, and prints response.Data as indented JSON to stdout.
preconditions: api_key is configured in config file
endpoint_url is configured
httptest.Server handles GET /user and returns a User JSON object
test_cases.input: Run akc user show against mock server
test_cases.expected: stdout contains indented User JSON with two-space indentation; exit code is 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_returns_user_json); setConfig(apiKey='ak_keyid_secret', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd('akc user show'); assert exitCode == 0; user = parse_json(stdout); assert user != nil; assert stdout contains '  ' (two-space indent pattern)

test_cases.description: Verify that user show exits with code 2 and the missing API key error JSON when api_key is absent, without making any network request.
preconditions: No api_key in config, environment, or flags
endpoint_url is configured
test_cases.input: Run akc user show with no api_key configured
test_cases.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":2,"message":"no API key configured — run 'akc login' first"}}; exit code is 2; mock server receives no requests
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_that_records_requests); clearApiKey(); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd('akc user show'); assert exitCode == 2; parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed['error']['code'] == 2; assert parsed['error']['message'] contains 'no API key configured'; assert server.requestCount == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that user update calls UpdateUser with the provided --full-name value and prints the updated User as indented JSON to stdout.
preconditions: api_key is configured
endpoint_url is configured
httptest.Server handles PATCH /user and returns updated User JSON
test_cases.input: Run akc user update --full-name 'Alice Smith' against mock server
test_cases.expected: Mock server receives PATCH request with body containing full_name='Alice Smith'; stdout contains updated User JSON; exit code is 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: capturedBody = nil; server = httptest.NewServer(func(req) { capturedBody = parse_json(req.Body); return updated_user }); setConfig(apiKey='ak_k_s', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd('akc user update --full-name "Alice Smith"'); assert exitCode == 0; assert capturedBody['full_name'] == 'Alice Smith'; user = parse_json(stdout); assert user != nil

test_cases.description: Verify that a 422 server error from user update surfaces as exit code 1 with the server's error envelope JSON to stdout.
preconditions: api_key is configured
endpoint_url is configured
httptest.Server handles PATCH /user and returns HTTP 422 with error envelope
test_cases.input: Run akc user update --full-name '' against mock server that returns 422
test_cases.expected: stdout contains the server's error envelope JSON; exit code is 1
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_returns_422_error_envelope); setConfig(apiKey='ak_k_s', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd('akc user update --full-name ""'); assert exitCode == 1; parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed['error'] != nil

test_cases.description: Verify that keys list calls ListKeys and prints the []*APIKeyMeta array as indented JSON to stdout.
preconditions: api_key is configured
endpoint_url is configured
httptest.Server handles GET /keys and returns a JSON array of APIKeyMeta objects
test_cases.input: Run akc keys list against mock server
test_cases.expected: stdout contains a JSON array of APIKeyMeta objects with two-space indentation; exit code is 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_returns_key_meta_array); setConfig(apiKey='ak_k_s', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd('akc keys list'); assert exitCode == 0; arr = parse_json(stdout); assert type(arr) == array

test_cases.description: Verify that keys refresh parses key_id, calls RefreshKey, updates config with the new api_key, prints APIKeyFull JSON to stdout, and prints the success message to stderr.
preconditions: api_key='ak_keyid123_secret' is configured
endpoint_url is configured
httptest.Server handles the refresh endpoint and returns APIKeyFull with key='ak_newkeyid_newsecret'
test_cases.input: Run akc keys refresh against mock server
test_cases.expected: Mock server receives refresh request with key_id='keyid123'; config api_key is updated to 'ak_newkeyid_newsecret'; stdout contains APIKeyFull JSON; stderr contains 'API key refreshed'; exit code is 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_returns_api_key_full); setConfig(apiKey='ak_keyid123_secret', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, stderr, exitCode = run_cmd('akc keys refresh'); assert exitCode == 0; config = load_config(); assert config['api_key'] == 'ak_newkeyid_newsecret'; keyFull = parse_json(stdout); assert keyFull != nil; assert stderr contains 'API key refreshed'

test_cases.description: Verify that keys refresh exits with code 2 and the invalid key format error JSON when the api_key has fewer than 3 underscore-delimited segments.
preconditions: api_key='badkey' (no underscores) is configured
endpoint_url is configured
test_cases.input: Run akc keys refresh with api_key='badkey'
test_cases.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":2,"message":"invalid API key format"}}; exit code is 2; no network request made
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_that_records_requests); setConfig(apiKey='badkey', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd('akc keys refresh'); assert exitCode == 2; parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed['error']['message'] == 'invalid API key format'; assert server.requestCount == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that keys revoke parses key_id, calls RevokeKey, clears api_key and user_id in config, prints RevokeKeyResponse JSON to stdout, and prints the revocation message to stderr.
preconditions: api_key='ak_keyid123_secret' and user_id='user-123' are configured
endpoint_url is configured
httptest.Server handles the revoke endpoint and returns RevokeKeyResponse
test_cases.input: Run akc keys revoke against mock server
test_cases.expected: Config api_key and user_id are set to empty strings; stdout contains RevokeKeyResponse JSON; stderr contains "API key revoked. Run 'akc login' to obtain a new key."; exit code is 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_returns_revoke_response); setConfig(apiKey='ak_keyid123_secret', userID='user-123', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, stderr, exitCode = run_cmd('akc keys revoke'); assert exitCode == 0; config = load_config(); assert config['api_key'] == ''; assert config['user_id'] == ''; revokeResp = parse_json(stdout); assert revokeResp != nil; assert stderr contains "API key revoked. Run 'akc login' to obtain a new key."

test_cases.description: Verify that keys revoke exits with code 2 and the invalid key format error JSON when the api_key has fewer than 3 underscore-delimited segments.
preconditions: api_key='onlyone' (no underscores) is configured
endpoint_url is configured
test_cases.input: Run akc keys revoke with api_key='onlyone'
test_cases.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":2,"message":"invalid API key format"}}; exit code is 2; no network request made
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_that_records_requests); setConfig(apiKey='onlyone', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd('akc keys revoke'); assert exitCode == 2; parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed['error']['message'] == 'invalid API key format'; assert server.requestCount == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that tokens list calls ListTokens and prints the []*PAT array as indented JSON to stdout.
preconditions: api_key is configured
endpoint_url is configured
httptest.Server handles GET /tokens and returns a JSON array of PAT objects
test_cases.input: Run akc tokens list against mock server
test_cases.expected: stdout contains a JSON array of PAT objects; exit code is 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_returns_pat_array); setConfig(apiKey='ak_k_s', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd('akc tokens list'); assert exitCode == 0; arr = parse_json(stdout); assert type(arr) == array

test_cases.description: Verify that tokens create parses permissions, validates expires, calls CreateToken, prints PATFull JSON to stdout, and prints the warning to stderr.
preconditions: api_key is configured
endpoint_url is configured
httptest.Server handles POST /tokens and returns PATFull JSON
test_cases.input: Run akc tokens create --name ci-bot --permissions 'users:read,orgs:read' --expires 30
test_cases.expected: Mock server receives POST with name='ci-bot', permissions=['users:read','orgs:read'], expires=30; stdout contains PATFull JSON; stderr contains the save-token warning; exit code is 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: capturedBody = nil; server = httptest.NewServer(func(req) { capturedBody = parse_json(req.Body); return pat_full }); setConfig(apiKey='ak_k_s', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, stderr, exitCode = run_cmd("akc tokens create --name ci-bot --permissions 'users:read,orgs:read' --expires 30"); assert exitCode == 0; assert capturedBody['name'] == 'ci-bot'; assert capturedBody['permissions'] == ['users:read', 'orgs:read']; assert capturedBody['expires'] == 30; assert parse_json(stdout) != nil; assert stderr contains 'Save the token value'

test_cases.description: Verify that tokens create exits with code 2 and the empty permissions error JSON when --permissions resolves to an empty slice.
preconditions: api_key is configured
endpoint_url is configured
test_cases.input: Run akc tokens create --name ci-bot --permissions '' --expires 90
test_cases.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":2,"message":"--permissions must not be empty"}}; exit code is 2; no network request made
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_that_records_requests); setConfig(apiKey='ak_k_s', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd("akc tokens create --name ci-bot --permissions '' --expires 90"); assert exitCode == 2; parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed['error']['message'] == '--permissions must not be empty'; assert server.requestCount == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that tokens create exits with code 2 and the expires validation error JSON when --expires is an invalid value.
preconditions: api_key is configured
endpoint_url is configured
test_cases.input: Run akc tokens create --name ci-bot --permissions 'users:read' --expires 15
test_cases.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":2,"message":"--expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90"}}; exit code is 2; no network request made
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_that_records_requests); setConfig(apiKey='ak_k_s', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd("akc tokens create --name ci-bot --permissions 'users:read' --expires 15"); assert exitCode == 2; parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed['error']['message'] == '--expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90'; assert server.requestCount == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that tokens show calls GetToken with the positional token_id argument and prints response.Data as indented JSON to stdout.
preconditions: api_key is configured
endpoint_url is configured
httptest.Server handles GET /tokens/{id} and returns Response[PAT] JSON
test_cases.input: Run akc tokens show tok-abc123 against mock server
test_cases.expected: Mock server receives GET request with tok-abc123 in the path; stdout contains PAT JSON; exit code is 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: capturedPath = ''; server = httptest.NewServer(func(req) { capturedPath = req.URL.Path; return response_pat }); setConfig(apiKey='ak_k_s', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd('akc tokens show tok-abc123'); assert exitCode == 0; assert 'tok-abc123' in capturedPath; pat = parse_json(stdout); assert pat != nil

test_cases.description: Verify that tokens revoke calls RevokeToken with the positional token_id, prints {} to stdout on success (HTTP 204), and prints the revocation message to stderr.
preconditions: api_key is configured
endpoint_url is configured
httptest.Server handles DELETE /tokens/{id} and returns HTTP 204 (no body)
test_cases.input: Run akc tokens revoke tok-abc123 against mock server
test_cases.expected: stdout contains exactly '{}'; stderr contains 'Token tok-abc123 revoked'; exit code is 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_returns_204); setConfig(apiKey='ak_k_s', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, stderr, exitCode = run_cmd('akc tokens revoke tok-abc123'); assert exitCode == 0; assert strip_whitespace(stdout) == '{}'; assert stderr contains 'Token tok-abc123 revoked'

test_cases.description: Verify that orgs list calls ListUserOrgs and prints the []*Organization array as indented JSON to stdout.

endpoint_url is configured
httptest.Server handles GET /orgs and returns a JSON array of Organization objects
test_cases.input: Run akc orgs list against mock server
test_cases.expected: stdout contains a JSON array of Organization objects; exit code is 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_returns_org_array); setConfig(apiKey='ak_k_s', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd('akc orgs list'); assert exitCode == 0; arr = parse_json(stdout); assert type(arr) == array

test_cases.description: Verify that orgs show calls GetOrg with the positional id argument and prints response.Data as indented JSON to stdout.
preconditions: api_key is configured
endpoint_url is configured
httptest.Server handles GET /orgs/{id} and returns Response[Organization] JSON
test_cases.input: Run akc orgs show org-uuid-abc against mock server
test_cases.expected: Mock server receives GET request with org-uuid-abc in the path; stdout contains Organization JSON; exit code is 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: capturedPath = ''; server = httptest.NewServer(func(req) { capturedPath = req.URL.Path; return response_org }); setConfig(apiKey='ak_k_s', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd('akc orgs show org-uuid-abc'); assert exitCode == 0; assert 'org-uuid-abc' in capturedPath; org = parse_json(stdout); assert org != nil

test_cases.description: Verify that orgs members calls ListOrgMembers with the positional id argument and prints the []*User array as indented JSON to stdout.

endpoint_url is configured
httptest.Server handles GET /orgs/{id}/members and returns a JSON array of User objects
test_cases.input: Run akc orgs members org-uuid-abc against mock server
test_cases.expected: Mock server receives GET request with org-uuid-abc in the path; stdout contains User array JSON; exit code is 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: capturedPath = ''; server = httptest.NewServer(func(req) { capturedPath = req.URL.Path; return user_array }); setConfig(apiKey='ak_k_s', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd('akc orgs members org-uuid-abc'); assert exitCode == 0; assert 'org-uuid-abc' in capturedPath; arr = parse_json(stdout); assert type(arr) == array

test_cases.description: Verify that parseKeyID correctly extracts the penultimate segment from various valid API key formats.
preconditions: parseKeyID is implemented in internal/cli/helpers.go
test_cases.input: Call parseKeyID with 'ak_abc12345_secret', 'myapp_abc12345_secret', 'my_app_abc12345_secret'
test_cases.expected: All three calls return 'abc12345' as key_id and nil error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: keyID, err = parseKeyID('ak_abc12345_secret'); assert err == nil; assert keyID == 'abc12345'; keyID, err = parseKeyID('myapp_abc12345_secret'); assert err == nil; assert keyID == 'abc12345'; keyID, err = parseKeyID('my_app_abc12345_secret'); assert err == nil; assert keyID == 'abc12345'

test_cases.description: Verify that parseKeyID returns an error when the api_key string has fewer than 3 underscore-delimited segments.
preconditions: parseKeyID is implemented in internal/cli/helpers.go
test_cases.input: Call parseKeyID with 'badkey', 'onlytwo_segments'
test_cases.expected: Both calls return an empty string and a non-nil error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: keyID, err = parseKeyID('badkey'); assert err != nil; assert keyID == ''; keyID, err = parseKeyID('onlytwo_segments'); assert err != nil; assert keyID == ''

test_cases.description: Verify that validateExpires returns nil for 0, 30, 60, 90 and a non-nil error for all other integers.
preconditions: validateExpires is implemented in internal/cli/helpers.go
test_cases.input: Call validateExpires with 0, 30, 60, 90, 1, 15, 45, 91, -1, 100
test_cases.expected: Returns nil for {0, 30, 60, 90}; returns non-nil error for all others
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for v in [0, 30, 60, 90]: assert validateExpires(v) == nil; for v in [1, 15, 45, 91, -1, 100]: err = validateExpires(v); assert err != nil; assert err.Error() contains '--expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90'

test_cases.description: Verify that parsePermissions correctly splits comma-separated input, trims whitespace, and returns a non-empty slice.
preconditions: parsePermissions is implemented in internal/cli/helpers.go
test_cases.input: Call parsePermissions with 'users:read,orgs:read', 'users:read, orgs:read', 'users:read'
test_cases.expected: Returns ["users:read","orgs:read"] for the first two inputs; returns ["users:read"] for the single-entry input
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: perms, err = parsePermissions('users:read,orgs:read'); assert err == nil; assert perms == ['users:read', 'orgs:read']; perms, err = parsePermissions('users:read, orgs:read'); assert err == nil; assert perms == ['users:read', 'orgs:read']; perms, err = parsePermissions('users:read'); assert err == nil; assert perms == ['users:read']

test_cases.description: Verify that parsePermissions returns an error with the expected message when input is empty or contains only whitespace and commas.
preconditions: parsePermissions is implemented in internal/cli/helpers.go
test_cases.input: Call parsePermissions with '' and '  , '
test_cases.expected: Both return nil slice and non-nil error with message '--permissions must not be empty'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: perms, err = parsePermissions(''); assert perms == nil; assert err != nil; assert err.Error() == '--permissions must not be empty'; perms, err = parsePermissions('  , '); assert perms == nil; assert err != nil; assert err.Error() == '--permissions must not be empty'

test_cases.description: Verify that openBrowser executes 'open' on darwin and 'xdg-open' on linux for the given URL.
preconditions: openBrowser is implemented in internal/cli/helpers.go
os/exec is used; test may stub exec.Command to capture invocation
test_cases.input: Call openBrowser with url='https://auth.example.com' on each platform (mocked GOOS)
test_cases.expected: On darwin: exec.Command called with 'open', 'https://auth.example.com'; on linux: exec.Command called with 'xdg-open', 'https://auth.example.com'; returns non-nil error if command fails
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: // Stub exec.Command to capture args; capturedCmd = nil; execCmd = func(name, args...) { capturedCmd = (name, args) }; openBrowserWithExec(execCmd, 'darwin', 'https://auth.example.com'); assert capturedCmd == ('open', 'https://auth.example.com'); openBrowserWithExec(execCmd, 'linux', 'https://auth.example.com'); assert capturedCmd == ('xdg-open', 'https://auth.example.com')

test_cases.description: Verify that all authenticated commands exit with code 2 and the missing API key error without making any network request when api_key is absent.
preconditions: No api_key in config, environment, or flags
endpoint_url is configured
httptest.Server is running and recording requests
test_cases.input: Run each authenticated command (user show, keys list, keys refresh, keys revoke, tokens list, tokens create, tokens show, tokens revoke, orgs list, orgs show, orgs members) without api_key
test_cases.expected: Each command exits with code 2; stdout contains {"error":{"code":2,"message":"no API key configured — run 'akc login' first"}}; mock server receives 0 requests
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_that_records_requests); clearApiKey(); for cmd in ['akc user show', 'akc keys list', 'akc tokens list', 'akc orgs list', ...]: stdout, exitCode = run_cmd(cmd); assert exitCode == 2; parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed['error']['message'] contains 'no API key configured'; assert server.requestCount == 0; server.requestCount = 0

test_cases.description: Verify that all commands format successful output using json.MarshalIndent with two-space indentation and no HTML escaping, writing to stdout, and write human-readable messages to stderr.
preconditions: Mock server returns a user object containing HTML-sensitive characters like '<', '>', '&'
api_key and endpoint_url are configured
test_cases.input: Run akc user show against mock server returning user with display_name='<Admin & Owner>'
test_cases.expected: stdout contains the display_name unescaped as '<Admin & Owner>' (not '\u003cAdmin \u0026 Owner\u003e'); output uses two-space indentation
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_returns_user_with_html_chars); setConfig(apiKey='ak_k_s', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd('akc user show'); assert exitCode == 0; assert '<Admin & Owner>' in stdout; assert '\u003c' not in stdout; lines = stdout.split('\n'); assert any(line.startswith('  ') for line in lines)

test_cases.description: Verify that a 401 server error from any command surfaces as exit code 1 with the server's error envelope JSON to stdout.
preconditions: api_key is configured
endpoint_url is configured
httptest.Server handles GET /user and returns HTTP 401 with error envelope JSON
test_cases.input: Run akc user show against mock server returning 401
test_cases.expected: stdout contains server's error envelope JSON; exit code is 1
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_returns_401); setConfig(apiKey='ak_k_s', endpointURL=server.URL); stdout, exitCode = run_cmd('akc user show'); assert exitCode == 1; parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed['error'] != nil

test_cases.description: Verify that config-mutating commands (login, keys refresh, keys revoke) use the CLI Core atomic write and preserve unchanged config fields.
preconditions: Config file contains endpoint_url, user_id, api_key, and an extra field 'custom_field'='preserved'
Mock server handles keys refresh endpoint
test_cases.input: Run akc keys refresh to trigger a config mutation
test_cases.expected: Config file is written atomically (temp file + rename); api_key is updated; custom_field='preserved' still present in config
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: setConfig(apiKey='ak_k_s', userID='u1', endpointURL='https://ep', customField='preserved'); server = httptest.NewServer(handler_returns_new_key); run_cmd('akc keys refresh'); config = load_config(); assert config['api_key'] != 'ak_k_s'; assert config['custom_field'] == 'preserved'; assert config['endpoint_url'] == 'https://ep'

test_cases.description: Verify that login.go defines loginTimeoutSeconds=120 as a package-level constant and that RunE calls runLogin with time.Duration(loginTimeoutSeconds)*time.Second.
preconditions: internal/cli/login.go is implemented
test_cases.input: Inspect the loginTimeoutSeconds constant and the RunE function of NewLoginCmd()
test_cases.expected: loginTimeoutSeconds == 120; runLogin is called with 120s timeout in RunE; runLogin accepts a timeout time.Duration parameter
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert loginTimeoutSeconds == 120; // Call runLogin directly with short timeout; err = runLogin(context.Background(), 50*ms, minimalOpts); assert err.Error() contains 'timed out'; // Verify constant unchanged after call; assert loginTimeoutSeconds == 120

property_tests.description: For any login invocation, the generated state parameter is exactly 64 lowercase hex characters derived from 32 bytes of crypto/rand output.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate N=100 state parameters by calling the state generation function
property_tests.invariant_check: Each generated state has len == 64; matches regex ^[0-9a-f]{64}$; no two consecutive calls return the same value

property_tests.description: For any OAuth authorization URL constructed by the login command, all existing query parameters from authorize_url are preserved and only redirect_uri, state, and response_type=code are added.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate random authorize_url strings with arbitrary sets of existing query parameters (including client_id, scope, and custom params)
property_tests.invariant_check: For each generated authorize_url: all original params appear unchanged in final URL; redirect_uri, state, response_type=code are present; client_id appears exactly once; no parameters are removed or overwritten

property_tests.description: For any HTTP request to the callback server at a path other than /callback, the server responds with HTTP 404 and neither codeCh nor errCh receives a value.
property_tests.invariant_check: For each path: HTTP response is 404; codeCh is empty after 20ms; errCh is empty after 20ms; server continues to accept further requests

property_tests.description: For any login flow where code exchange succeeds but config write fails, stdout contains only the error envelope and no credential data.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary valid ExchangeOAuthCode responses (varying user.id, username, api_key.key values) with config write always failing
property_tests.invariant_check: stdout does not contain any of: the generated user.id, username, api_key.key values; stdout parses as a JSON error envelope with code 2

property_tests.description: parseKeyID always returns the penultimate underscore-delimited segment regardless of the number of underscores in the prefix.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate api_key strings of the form <prefix>_<keyid>_<secret> where prefix may contain 0 to 5 underscores, keyid is alphanumeric, secret is alphanumeric
property_tests.invariant_check: parseKeyID returns the keyid segment (penultimate) and nil error for all generated strings with >= 3 segments; error is returned for strings with < 3 segments

property_tests.description: For any invocation of any authenticated command without a configured api_key, no network request is issued and the command exits with code 2 before any SDK method is called.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Enumerate all authenticated commands (user show, user update, keys list, keys refresh, keys revoke, tokens list, tokens create, tokens show, tokens revoke, orgs list, orgs show, orgs members) with no api_key configured
property_tests.invariant_check: For each command: mock server request count remains 0; exit code is 2; stdout contains error envelope with 'no API key configured'

property_tests.description: validateExpires accepts exactly {0, 30, 60, 90} and rejects all other integers.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Test all integers in range [-10, 100] and a selection of large values (e.g., 1000, -1000)
property_tests.invariant_check: validateExpires(v) == nil if and only if v in {0, 30, 60, 90}; for all other tested values, validateExpires returns non-nil error containing '--expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90'

property_tests.description: The callback server shutdown always uses a fresh context derived from context.Background() with a 5-second deadline, regardless of the login context state.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Trigger login flow termination via all three paths: success, timeout, and state mismatch
property_tests.invariant_check: In each termination path: the context passed to http.Server.Shutdown is not the login context; the context has a deadline between now and now+5s; the context is not already Done

property_tests.description: For any successful akc tokens revoke invocation, stdout contains exactly the JSON object {} regardless of SDK response content.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Run tokens revoke with various token_id values against mock servers returning HTTP 204
property_tests.invariant_check: strip_whitespace(stdout) == '{}'; parse_json(stdout) is a valid empty object; exit code is 0

property_tests.description: loginTimeoutSeconds remains 120 throughout all invocations of the login command and runLogin.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Call runLogin multiple times with short timeouts (simulating tests) and inspect loginTimeoutSeconds after each call
property_tests.invariant_check: loginTimeoutSeconds == 120 before, during, and after every runLogin call regardless of outcome (timeout, success, error)

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that when the config write fails after successful code exchange, the login command exits with code 2 with the config failure error JSON and does NOT print credentials to stdout.
preconditions: Mock server returns a valid ExchangeOAuthCode response
CLI Core atomic write function is stubbed to return an error
endpoint_url and provider are configured
edge_case_tests.input: Complete full login flow with config save stubbed to fail
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":2,"message":"failed to save config: <detail>"}}; no user JSON or api_key value appears in stdout; exit code is 2
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: saveConfigFn = func(...) { return error('disk full') }; stdout, exitCode = run_login_with_save_stub(saveConfigFn); assert exitCode == 2; parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed['error']['code'] == 2; assert parsed['error']['message'] contains 'failed to save config'; assert 'api_key' not in stdout; assert 'username' not in stdout

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a request to a non-/callback path (e.g., /favicon.ico) returns HTTP 404 and does not signal codeCh or errCh, leaving the server running.
preconditions: Callback server is running with a known state
codeCh and errCh channels are created with buffer of 1
edge_case_tests.input: Send GET /favicon.ico to the callback server
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP response status is 404; codeCh is empty; errCh is empty; server continues listening on the port
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: codeCh = make(chan string, 1); errCh = make(chan error, 1); server, addr = startCallbackServer('validstate', codeCh, errCh); resp = http_get('http://' + addr + '/favicon.ico'); assert resp.StatusCode == 404; sleep(10ms); select { case <-codeCh: fail('unexpected code'); case <-errCh: fail('unexpected error'); default: pass }; // verify server still responds; resp2 = http_get('http://' + addr + '/favicon.ico'); assert resp2.StatusCode == 404

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that when loginTimeoutSeconds elapses (using short timeout in test) without a valid /callback, runLogin returns a timeout error and the callback server is shut down.
preconditions: runLogin is called directly with a 100ms timeout
Mock server is configured for GetProviders but no callback is sent
edge_case_tests.input: Call runLogin with timeout=100ms, no callback sent within timeout
edge_case_tests.expected: runLogin returns error with message 'login timed out waiting for browser callback'; callback server is no longer accepting connections after return
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: opts = loginOpts{provider: 'github', expires: 90, endpointURL: mockServer.URL}; err = runLogin(context.Background(), 100*ms, opts); assert err != nil; assert err.Error() contains 'login timed out waiting for browser callback'; // Verify server is shut down; resp = http_get_with_timeout(callbackAddr, 50ms); assert resp is connection_refused_error

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that when openBrowser fails, the fallback URL is printed to stderr and the login flow continues without treating the failure as fatal.
preconditions: openBrowser is replaced with a stub returning an error
stderr is captured
edge_case_tests.input: Run login flow with openBrowser stub returning an error
edge_case_tests.expected: stderr contains 'Open this URL in your browser: <url>'; login flow does not exit early due to browser open failure
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: stderrBuf = buffer(); openBrowserStub = func(url) { return error('no display') }; // inject stub into login flow; runLoginWithBrowserStub(stderrBuf, openBrowserStub, endpointURL=mockServer.URL); assert stderrBuf contains 'Open this URL in your browser:'; assert stderrBuf contains 'https://'

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that the callback server shutdown always uses a fresh context.Background() with a 5-second timeout even when the login context is already cancelled.
preconditions: Login flow reaches shutdown step with the login context already cancelled (e.g., after state mismatch)
edge_case_tests.input: Trigger state mismatch to cancel login context, then observe server shutdown context
edge_case_tests.expected: http.Server.Shutdown is called with a context derived from context.Background() (not the cancelled login context); server shuts down successfully within 5 seconds
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: capturedShutdownCtx = nil; serverShutdownFn = func(ctx) { capturedShutdownCtx = ctx; return nil }; trigger_state_mismatch_and_capture_shutdown(serverShutdownFn); assert capturedShutdownCtx != nil; deadline, ok = capturedShutdownCtx.Deadline(); assert ok == true; remaining = deadline - now(); assert remaining > 0 and remaining <= 5000ms

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that when the config write fails after a successful RefreshKey response, keys refresh exits with code 2 with a config failure error envelope and does NOT print the new key.
preconditions: api_key='ak_keyid_secret' is configured
Mock server returns APIKeyFull successfully
CLI Core atomic write is stubbed to fail after keys refresh
edge_case_tests.input: Run akc keys refresh with config save stubbed to fail
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains config-failure error envelope; exit code is 2; new key value NOT in stdout
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_returns_api_key_full); setConfig(apiKey='ak_keyid_secret', endpointURL=server.URL); saveConfigStub = func(...) { return error('io error') }; stdout, exitCode = run_keys_refresh_with_save_stub(saveConfigStub); assert exitCode == 2; parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed['error']['code'] == 2; assert 'ak_newkeyid_newsecret' not in stdout

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that when the CLI Core atomic write fails during any config-mutating command, the command exits with code 2 and the data that would have been printed on success is NOT printed.
preconditions: api_key is configured for keys revoke scenario
Mock server returns RevokeKeyResponse successfully
CLI Core atomic write is stubbed to return an error
edge_case_tests.input: Run akc keys revoke with config save stubbed to fail
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains config-failure error envelope; exit code is 2; RevokeKeyResponse JSON NOT in stdout
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: server = httptest.NewServer(handler_returns_revoke_response); setConfig(apiKey='ak_keyid_secret', endpointURL=server.URL); saveConfigStub = func(...) { return error('permission denied') }; stdout, exitCode = run_keys_revoke_with_save_stub(saveConfigStub); assert exitCode == 2; parsed = parse_json(stdout); assert parsed['error']['code'] == 2; assert 'revoked' not in stdout or parsed['error'] != nil

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test of the full login OAuth flow: providers fetched, browser opened, callback received with valid state, code exchanged, config saved, user JSON printed to stdout.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run akc login --provider github --expires 90 --endpoint-url <mockServerURL> in a test environment with simulated browser callback
mockable: httptest.Server replacing the apikit server (handles GET /providers and POST /auth/callback)
openBrowser replaced with a no-op stub
Simulated browser callback via direct HTTP request to callback server with correct state and code
expected_effects: GET /providers is called on the mock server
Authorization URL is constructed with redirect_uri, state, response_type=code preserving existing params
POST to exchange endpoint is called with correct provider, code, redirect_uri, expires
Config file atomically updated with endpoint_url, user_id, api_key
Callback server is shut down via fresh 5-second background context

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for akc user show happy path: authenticated client constructed, GetUser called, user JSON printed to stdout.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run akc user show with api_key and endpoint_url configured pointing to a mock server
mockable: httptest.Server handling GET /user returning a User JSON object
expected_effects: GET /user is called on the mock server with authentication headers

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for akc keys refresh: key_id parsed from api_key, RefreshKey called, config updated with new key, APIKeyFull JSON printed to stdout.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run akc keys refresh with api_key='ak_keyid123_secret' configured, pointing to mock server
real_components: CLI Core NewAuthenticatedClient and atomic write (spec 13)
mockable: httptest.Server handling the refresh endpoint returning APIKeyFull with key='ak_newkeyid_newsecret'
expected_effects: Config api_key is atomically updated to 'ak_newkeyid_newsecret'

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for login timeout: runLogin called with 100ms timeout and no callback sent, command exits with timeout error.
smoke_tests.trigger: Call runLogin directly with timeout=100ms, mock server returns providers but no callback is triggered
mockable: openBrowser replaced with no-op stub (no actual browser opened)
expected_effects: runLogin returns error containing 'login timed out waiting for browser callback'
Exit code is 2 when surfaced through command
stdout contains timeout error envelope JSON
Callback server is shut down after timeout

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for akc tokens create: permissions parsed, expires validated, CreateToken called with correct request body, PATFull JSON printed to stdout.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run akc tokens create --name ci-bot --permissions 'users:read,orgs:read' --expires 30 with api_key configured
mockable: httptest.Server handling POST /tokens returning PATFull JSON with plaintext token
expected_effects: CreateToken is called with name='ci-bot', permissions=['users:read','orgs:read'], expires=30
stdout contains indented PATFull JSON including plaintext token

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for pre-validation firing before network: running akc orgs list without api_key exits with code 2 and no HTTP request is made.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run akc orgs list with no api_key in config, env, or flags, with endpoint_url pointing to a mock server
expected_effects: stdout contains {"error":{"code":2,"message":"no API key configured — run 'akc login' first"}}
